techniques.evidence_quote: The issue may be related to a third-party ad provider
In some cases, turning off JavaScript or using an ad-blocker could help thwart the code
somebody has programmed the site to run multiple versions of Coin Hive simultaneously, basically bringing any visitor's computer to a processing halt

attack_chain.description: Attacker gained foothold on PolitiFact's website, likely through a misconfigured cloud server or a compromised third-party ad provider, injecting malicious Coinhive JavaScript into the site's served content.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: The issue may be related to a third-party ad provider

attack_chain.description: Malicious Coinhive JavaScript code was embedded into PolitiFact's website pages, executing automatically in visitors' browsers upon page load.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: visiting Politifact.com caused his computer's CPU to run at its maximum capacity

attack_chain.description: Multiple simultaneous instances of Coinhive were run against visitors' browsers, hijacking their CPU resources to mine Monero cryptocurrency without knowledge or consent.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: somebody has programmed the site to run multiple versions of Coin Hive simultaneously, basically bringing any visitor's computer to a processing halt

initial_access_vector: Misconfigured cloud server or compromised third-party ad provider used to inject Coinhive script into PolitiFact's website
persistence_mechanism: Coinhive JavaScript embedded in website pages, executing on every visitor session until removed
